sidebar.title: vfox
sidebar.scanHint: Select a project directory to auto-detect required SDKs

project.noHistory: No version switch history yet. Switch an SDK version in project mode and history will appear here.
project.pickDirectory: Select project directory — .tool-versions will be written here

snapshot.saveHint: Save current SDK versions for one-click restore. Single SDK snapshots only affect that SDK; full snapshots restore everything.
snapshot.saveSdkHint: Only saves the selected {{sdk}}; restore affects only it
snapshot.pickSdkHint: Select an SDK in the sidebar first
snapshot.saveAllHint: Save all installed SDK versions; restore replays the full environment
snapshot.restoreMessage: This will switch SDK versions back to when the snapshot was saved. Single SDK snapshots affect only that SDK; full snapshots restore all SDKs.

update.autoRestart: App will restart automatically after completion

confirm.uninstallMessage: Are you sure you want to uninstall this version? This action cannot be undone.
confirm.removePluginMessage: Remove this SDK plugin? Installed versions will remain on disk.
confirm.restoreSnapshotMessage: This will restore SDK versions to the snapshot state.

settings.title: Settings
settings.themeHint: Choose light, dark, or follow system
settings.checkAppHint: See if a new version of vfox-gui is available
settings.updateVfoxHint: Update the vfox command-line tool to the latest version

help.title: Help
help.installVersionDesc: Select an SDK from the sidebar, find the version you want in the 'Available' list, and click 'Install'. Progress and speed are shown during download.
help.switchVersionDesc: Click 'Switch' next to an installed version to make it the global default for that SDK. Switches take effect immediately.
help.uninstallVersionDesc: Click 'Uninstall' to open a confirmation dialog. Once confirmed, the version is permanently removed.
help.projectScopeDesc: Toggle the 'Global / Project' switch to 'Project', pick a directory. Version switches write a <0>.tool-versions</0> file that only affects the project. Other SDK entries are preserved; only the switched one is updated. Each switch is also logged to <1>.vfox-history.json</1>.
help.snapshotsDesc: Use the sidebar 'Snapshots' panel to save SDK version combos. 'Save Current SDK' saves only the selected SDK (e.g. <0>java@25</0>); restoring touches just it. 'Save All' saves every SDK; restoring replays the full environment.
help.scanProjectDesc: Cloned a project but not sure what to install? Click 'Scan Project' and pick the directory. It auto-detects required SDKs: <0>package.json</0>→Node.js, <1>go.mod</1>→Go, <2>Cargo.toml</2>→Rust, <3>requirements.txt</3>→Python, etc. Detected SDKs have an 'Add' button for one-click install.
help.escDesc: Dismiss current popup, dialog, or context menu
help.faq1q: Version switched but terminal still shows old version?
help.faq1a: Open a <0>new</0> terminal window — existing ones don't auto-refresh. If new windows don't work either, install the shell hook: <1>vfox add <shell></1> (e.g. vfox add bash / powershell).
help.faq2a: Most likely a corrupted download or interrupted extraction. Uninstall the broken version in the GUI and reinstall. Also check 'Update vfox CLI' to ensure you're on the latest.
help.faq3a: Fixed in the latest version. If a leftover remains, it means the version directory is corrupted — the GUI will auto-clean it.
help.faq4q: I see 'current shell lacks hook support'?
help.faq4a: Just ignore it. The GUI process can't have a shell hook, so vfox automatically falls back to global scope. Everything works fine.
help.footer: vfox-gui is a lightweight GUI for the vfox version manager.

empty.noVfoxDesc: Please install vfox first. Once installed, verify that vfox available works in your terminal, then click 'Recheck'.
empty.selectSdk: Select an SDK from the sidebar
